terms.definition: A finite period during which a complex system undergoes a first-order change of state, producing new equilibrium conditions
A cluster of interconnected crises whose combined impact exceeds the sum of individual shocks
The average number of live births a woman would have over her lifetime given current age-specific fertility rates
Decision-making power justified by specialized, credential-based expertise rather than by tradition or popular will
The perceived divergence between official statements and observable reality, leading to distrust in authorities
A permanent shift in the parameters of a statistical time series, indicating a regime change
A cultural orientation that interprets personal and social problems primarily through a psychological or medical lens
Money that derives its value from government decree rather than from a commodity backing such as gold

terms.definition: A system of moral norms justified by institutional or individual assertion rather than by external, transcendental standards
